RULING; DEFENDANT IN PRO PER MOVING PARTY NOT ALLOWED TO ARGUE THE MATTER DUE TO COMPLIANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING RULE, HENCE, the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- DENY-FAILED TO SUSTAIN BURDEN.Prevailing party to prepare order.(302/REQ/CK)BELDON YEE # 2647

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-13-2005 Continued To Apr-18-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-18-2005 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on as re-assigned by the Master Calendar Dept. 206 for Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Teena Paxton.
Michael Rossoff, Esq. for Plaintiff: Tenderloin Neighborhood Development Corp.
No appearance by the Defendant: Richard Brandy.
Plaintiff's witness: Theresa Forest, was sworn and direct examined.
Exhibit 1 - Rental Agreement------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 2 - Three day notice------------------ID/EVID.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant as follows:
Restitution and possession of the premises located at 34 Turk St., #437, San Francisco.
Rent and Per Diem Damages of $1,287.00
Attorney's Fees $375.00
Costs waived.
Rental Agreement declared forfeited.
Judgment signed in open court.
Counsel stipulated for the return of the Exhibits in open court.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2005 Assigned To Trial Court
